Events.title: Zest Fest 2026
Events.description: 🎉 Zest Fest 2026 Where energy meets vibes and memories are made 💃🕺 Get ready to turn up the heat because Zest Fest 2026 is here to bring the colour, chaos, and charisma to campus! 🌈🔥 Whether you’re coming for the music, the games, the food, or just the main character energy, this is THE event you don’t want to miss. Expect a full day of: 🎶 High-energy performances 🎯 Interactive games & booths 🍔 Food that slaps 📸 Photo-worthy moments 🎁 Giveaways & surprises This is your chance to let loose, meet new people, and vibe with the TP community like never before. Come with your friends, come solo, come ready to serve looks and collect memories — because Zest Fest is all about fun, freedom, and full-on flavour. ✨ Zesty? Yes. ✨ Iconic? Absolutely. ✨ Missable? NEVER. ⏰ Registration Closing Date 🗓 Registration closes on: 10 February 2026 🚨 Spots are limited, bestie — once it’s full, it’s FULL. 💥 Register now and secure your spot at the most zesty event of 2026. Because if you weren’t at Zest Fest… were you even there? 😌🔥

Events.title: GoalsMapper Conference 2026
Events.description: 🎯 Goals Mapper 2026 Turning ambition into action Goals Mapper 2026 is an inspiring and practical event designed to help students gain clarity, direction, and confidence in planning their personal, academic, and career goals. Led by CEO Wayne Dato and CEO Colin Pok, this session will guide participants through proven strategies to: Identify meaningful short-term and long-term goals Break big ambitions into actionable steps Overcome common obstacles such as uncertainty and lack of direction Build a structured roadmap for success Through real-world insights, interactive discussions, and guided reflection, attendees will learn how successful individuals map their goals and stay accountable throughout their journey. Whether you are still exploring your path or already working towards a clear vision, Goals Mapper 2026 provides valuable tools to help you move forward with purpose and intention. ✨ Your goals matter. ✨ Your direction matters. ✨ This is where the mapping begins. Registrations ends 5 January 2026. DO SIGN UP SPOTS ARE LIMITED

Events.title: Pok Pok Daily Lectures
Events.description: Pok Pok Daily Lectures is a unique and engaging science lecture series led by Colin Pok, a passionate science educator and researcher from the School of Applied Science. Designed to break away from traditional long-form lectures, this event delivers short, high-impact daily sessions that aim to spark curiosity, challenge assumptions, and make science both accessible and enjoyable for students of all backgrounds. Over the course of several days, participants will be exposed to a wide range of fascinating scientific concepts — from everyday phenomena explained through physics and chemistry, to emerging discoveries in applied science and technology. Each lecture is carefully structured to be concise yet thought-provoking, encouraging students to think critically, ask questions, and actively engage rather than passively listen. Colin Pok is known for his energetic teaching style and ability to simplify complex scientific ideas without losing their depth. His sessions often include real-world examples, live demonstrations, interactive quizzes, and short discussions to keep the momentum going. Whether it’s explaining why certain reactions behave unexpectedly, or how scientific thinking applies beyond the lab, each lecture promises something new and surprising. Logistics and Important Information: Venue: Centre for Aquaculture and Veterinary Science Block 8A Temasek Polytechnic Format: A lecture of 1 hour will commence along with 30mins of QNA and networking with the man the myth the legend Colin himself Recommended Arrival: Join the session 10 minutes early to ensure best experience Students are encouraged to attend all sessions in the series to gain the most value, as concepts introduced earlier may be referenced or expanded upon in later lectures. While no prior advanced science knowledge is required, an open and curious mindset is highly recommended. Who Should Attend: Students from Applied Science and other schools interested in science Anyone curious about how science explains the world around us Students looking to earn participation points while learning something genuinely interesting. Due to limited capacity and high demand, registration may close once maximum attendance is reached. If you are successfully registered, please make a commitment to attend, as no-shows may prevent others from participating. In short, Pok Pok Daily Lectures is not just another event — it’s a bite-sized scientific journey designed to inform, inspire, and ignite curiosity. Come with questions, leave with insights, and maybe even a newfound appreciation for the science behind everyday life.
Events.venue: Centre for Aquaculture and Veterinary Science Block 8A Temasek Polytechnic

Events.title: TP Open House - IIT Facilitation
Events.description:
TP Open House - IIT Facilitation

Be the first face that welcomes future TP students

The TP Open House – IIT Facilitation event invites students to be part of the team that helps make Temasek Polytechnic’s Open House a smooth, welcoming, and memorable experience for all guests.

As an IIT Facilitator, you will play an important role in:

🙋‍♂️ Ushering guests around the IIT school and event areas

🗺️ Providing directions and basic information to visitors

😊 Assisting prospective students and parents with enquiries

🎤 Supporting staff and lecturers during Open House activities

This is a great opportunity to:

Gain real-world event facilitation experience

Develop communication and people skills

Contribute meaningfully to a major TP event

Represent IIT and TP with pride

No prior experience is required — just a positive attitude, willingness to help, and confidence in interacting with the public.

⏰ Registration Closing Date

🗓 Registration closes on: 7 January

Limited slots available. Sign up early to secure your role and be part of the team that shapes first impressions at TP Open House!
Events.venue: IIT App Experience Hub for assembly

Events.title: Made in Singapore, design fashion stories 2026
Events.description:
Made in Singapore: Design & Fashion Stories 2026

Celebrating local creativity, identity, and style

Made in Singapore: Design & Fashion Stories 2026 is a curated fashion and design event that shines a spotlight on Singapore’s creative talents, stories, and evolving design identity.

Set in the heart of Singapore’s fashion scene, this event brings together designers, creatives, and enthusiasts to explore how local culture, innovation, and storytelling shape modern fashion. Through showcases, talks, and interactive segments, attendees will gain insight into the journeys behind Singapore-made designs — from concept to creation.

Participants can expect:

✨ Showcases of locally inspired fashion and design works

🎤 Sharing sessions from creatives and designers

🧵 Stories behind materials, craftsmanship, and identity

🤝 Opportunities to connect with the local creative community

Whether you are passionate about fashion, design, or creative industries, this event offers a deeper appreciation of what it truly means to create Made in Singapore.

📍 Venue

Design Orchard, Orchard Road
A vibrant creative space where fashion, design, and local talent thrive in the heart of Singapore.

Events.title: Christian Bible Studies 2025
Events.description: Understanding God's word in a deeper way like never before. This is the word that changed and revolutionised the world, let us trust and put our faith in it

Events.title: TPSU presents: Temasek Polytechnic Inter Frisbee Games 2026
Events.description:
🥏 TPSU Presents: Temasek Polytechnic Inter Frisbee Games 2026

Fast throws, sharp teamwork, unbeatable spirit

Get ready to bring your A-game because TPSU presents: Temasek Polytechnic Inter Frisbee Games 2026 is where speed, strategy, and teamwork collide on the field.

This inter-school friendly competition brings together students from across TP to compete, connect, and showcase their skills in an exciting and fast-paced frisbee tournament. Whether you’re an experienced player or just passionate about team sports, this event promises intense matches, great sportsmanship, and unforgettable moments.

Participants can look forward to:

🥏 Competitive yet friendly frisbee matches

🤝 Team-based gameplay that builds coordination and trust

💪 A fun way to stay active and meet fellow sports enthusiasts

🎉 A vibrant sporting atmosphere powered by TPSU

Come ready to play hard, support your teammates, and enjoy the thrill of the game.

⏰ Registration Closing Date

🗓 Registration closes on: 10 January

Slots are limited — sign up early and be part of one of TP’s most exciting sporting events of 2026!

Events.title: How to not get kicked out of flying cadet school
Events.description:
✈️ How to Not Get Kicked Out of Flying Cadet School

Lessons from discipline, resilience, and real expectations

How to Not Get Kicked Out of Flying Cadet School is an informative sharing session focused on helping aspiring cadets understand the realities, expectations, and common pitfalls of flying cadet training.

This session breaks down what flying cadet schools truly look for beyond technical ability — including discipline, attitude, teamwork, time management, and mental resilience. Through practical examples and real experiences, participants gain insight into why cadets struggle, where many go wrong, and how to adapt early to demanding training environments.

Key takeaways from the session include:

Understanding the discipline and standards expected in cadet training

Common mistakes that lead to dismissal and how to avoid them

Managing stress, pressure, and fatigue effectively

Building the right mindset for long-term success

Learning how attitude and professionalism impact progression

This event provides a grounded and honest perspective for students who are curious about aviation pathways or considering flying cadet programmes, helping them make informed decisions and better prepare for the challenges ahead.

Events.title: Beach Clean Up
Events.description:
Hey TP! 👋
The Informatics & IT Studies Club  and the TP Sustainability Interest Group have come together to bring you a Beach Clean-Up! 🏖️♻️

Pre event briefing details:
📅 Date: 2 January 2026
⏰ Time: 6pm 
📍 Location: Online 


Actual event details:
📅 Date: 3 January 2026 
⏰ Time: 9:30 AM – 12:00 PM
📍 Location: Changi Beach Area A 

Join us as we team up to make a real difference for our environment 💚🌍 ✨ 

What’s in store:
🤝 Team bonding
🌊 Giving back to nature
♻️ Meaningful impact 

All participants will be awarded seal points!

🔗 sign ups close on the 30th of December, so do sign up now!

Events.title: Movie night with Strangers
Events.description:
🎬 Movie Night with Strangers

Good food, great films, and new connections

Movie Night with Strangers is a relaxed social event designed to bring people together through a shared love for movies, good conversations, and a welcoming atmosphere.

The evening begins with dinner at 5:00 PM, giving participants a chance to mingle, chat, and get comfortable before settling in for the movie screening. Whether you come with friends or on your own, this event creates an easygoing space to meet new people and enjoy a casual night out.

What to expect:

🍽️ Dinner served at 5:00 PM

🎥 A curated movie screening

💬 Light conversations and social interaction

😊 A friendly, inclusive environment for everyone

This event isn’t about forced interactions — it’s about enjoying a film together, sharing a meal, and letting connections happen naturally. Perfect for those looking to unwind, meet new faces, or simply enjoy a movie in good company.
